purpose: Gates that fire whenever a token's reading turns on a hamza — as a RADICAL (أ/ء in the root) or as a SEAT (إ/أ/ؤ/ئ/آ orthographic carrier). norm() drops every hamza seat and all harakāt, so it routinely merges words that differ only by seat (إيمان/أيمان, آمن/أمن). These gates name the collapses norm() causes and require independent certification before any such token may be authored. A gate firing means: do NOT trust norm(); certify with norm_strict() AND the QAC root AND (when present) the Qamus entry. If the distinguishing seat/harakah is absent in the source, emit pending — never the commoner reading. Public artifacts stay {src:'qamus', kind:'authored', lang:'en'}; QAC is INTERNAL evidence and is never exposed.

fields.trigger: what makes this gate fire on a token
fields.role: radical (hamza belongs to the root) | seat (orthographic carrier, not a radical) | both
fields.rule: the operational test on the diacritized surface, naming the helper/letter/harakah
fields.norm_merges: the distinct words norm() wrongly fuses
fields.correct: the defensible reading, or 'pending' when the seat/harakah is absent
fields.example_loc: a quran:S:A:W where the correct reading holds
fields.certify_with: the rungs that must agree before authoring

seat_inventory.note: Hamza is written on one of several seats whose choice depends on the surrounding vowels. norm_strict() PRESERVES the seat; norm() ERASES it. The seats and their bearer letters:

seats.hamzat_qatʿ_alif: أ (fatḥa/ḍamma) / إ (kasra) — initial connecting/cutting hamza on alif
seats.hamzat_wasl: ٱ — elidable alif, NOT a hamza-qaṭʿ; carries no glottal stop in continuous reading
seats.madda: آ — alif + hamza + long-ā fused; expands to أَا/أْا underlyingly
seats.yaʾ_seat: ئ — hamza on a dotless/notched yā' (kasra context)
seats.waw_seat: ؤ — hamza on a wāw (ḍamma context)
seats.standalone: ء — hamza on the line (after a long vowel / certain medials)

decide_on_seat: Read the orthographic seat AND the harakah on the first content letter (the seat may follow a وَ/فَ/لِ proclitic). A bare alif with no visible hamza-seat or harakah is ambiguous between hamzat-qaṭʿ and hamzat-waṣl; if the source does not disambiguate, the gate yields pending. Never let norm() — which has already discarded the seat — be the tiebreaker.

norm_collapses.rule: إِيمَان: initial hamza-kasra (إ) on root أ م ن, the ي is a glide (madd) → 'faith / belief'. أَيْمَان: initial hamza-fatḥa (أ) on root ي م ن, the ي is a RADICAL → 'oaths' (plural of يَمِين). norm() drops both the seat and the harakah and fuses them.
norm_collapses.correct: faith / belief — or 'oaths' per the seat; pending if the seat is absent

norm_collapses.trigger: skeleton ا+م+ن verb after norm(), seat undetermined
norm_collapses.rule: آمَنَ (madda seat آ = Form IV أَأْمَنَ contracted) → 'to believe / have faith' (root أ م ن). أَمِنَ (plain hamza-fatḥa أ) → 'to be safe / secure' (Form I, same root, different sense). أَمَّنَ (shadda on mīm) → 'to grant safety'. The madda seat is the Form-IV signal; norm() erases it and the shadda.
norm_collapses.norm_merges: آمَنَ (believed, IV) ⇄ أَمِنَ (was safe, I) ⇄ أَمَّنَ (gave safety, II)
norm_collapses.wrong_gloss: to be safe (when the token is آمَنَ 'believed')
norm_collapses.correct: to believe (madda) / to be safe (plain) / to grant safety (shadda) per the seat+shadda; pending if undiacritized

norm_collapses.trigger: any token beginning with آ (madda) whose Form-IV reading is in question
norm_collapses.rule: Initial آ frequently encodes a contracted Form-IV أَأ (آتَى ← أَأْتَى 'to give', آمَنَ ← أَأْمَنَ). norm() flattens آ to a single alif, hiding the Form-IV augment and inviting a Form-I gloss. Restore the underlying أَأ via norm_strict() before deciding the wazn.
norm_collapses.correct: gave / brought (Form IV) — or 'came' (Form I) per the madda; pending if the seat is lost

norm_collapses.trigger: initial ٱ (alif-waṣl) mistaken for a hamza-qaṭʿ radical
norm_collapses.rule: Hamzat al-waṣl (ٱ) is an elidable connector, NOT a radical and NOT a hamza-qaṭʿ. In ٱسْتَغْفَرَ (Form X) and ٱلْكِتَاب (article) the initial alif carries no glottal stop in continuous reading. Do not count it as a first radical hamza; the root of ٱسْتَغْفَرَ is غ ف ر.
norm_collapses.norm_merges: ٱسْم (waṣl, 'name', root س م و/و س م) treated as a hamza-initial root أ س م
norm_collapses.correct: strip the waṣl; certify the true root (غ ف ر, س م و, …) via the root ladder

norm_collapses.trigger: a medial hamza whose seat (ئ/ؤ/ء) shifts with case/mood inflection
norm_collapses.rule: A radical hamza changes seat by harakah: سَأَلَ (أ) ⇄ يَسْـئَلُ/يَسْأَلُ ⇄ سُئِلَ (ئ). The seat shift is orthographic; the radical is constant (root س أ ل). norm() drops every seat, so it cannot track that these are one root inflecting — author the root once, not three.
norm_collapses.norm_merges: سَأَلَ ⇄ سُئِلَ ⇄ يَسْأَلُونَ as if different stems
norm_collapses.wrong_gloss: split senses for one inflecting root
norm_collapses.correct: single root س أ ل ('to ask'); the seat is a spelling rule of the vowel context

norm_collapses.trigger: a terminal ـَاء that may be a radical hamza OR a feminine/plural marker
norm_collapses.rule: Terminal ـَاء is a RADICAL hamza in some roots (مَاء root م و ه/م ي ه via assimilation; سَمَاء root س م و) but a FEMININE/PLURAL marker in others (شُهَدَاء plural of شَهِيد, root ش ه د — the hamza is NOT a radical). norm() cannot tell them apart. Check whether the third radical is genuinely hamza before certifying it.
norm_collapses.norm_merges: شُهَدَاء (plural marker) ⇄ a fabricated hamza-final root
norm_collapses.wrong_gloss: a hamza third-radical root for a sound plural
norm_collapses.correct: decide via the singular + QAC root: plural-marker hamza ≠ radical hamza

norm_collapses.trigger: ئ (yā'-seat hamza) vs a true long-ī glide after norm()
norm_collapses.rule: أَئِمَّة (yā'-seat hamza, root أ م م) 'leaders/imams' vs a glide-ī reading. The ئ encodes a hamza in a kasra context, NOT a long vowel. norm() turns ئ into a plain yā', erasing the hamza and the shadda on the mīm.
norm_collapses.wrong_gloss: a vowel-glide reading with no hamza
norm_collapses.correct: leaders / imams (root أ م م), hamza on the yā'-seat; pending if seat+shadda absent

gates.when: a token's gloss depends on which hamza seat is present (any norm_collapses entry could match)
gates.then: do NOT author from norm(); require norm_strict() to preserve the seat AND QAC root agreement AND (if it exists) a Qamus entry under norm_strict.

gates.when: the source surface lacks the disambiguating seat or harakah (bare alif, no madda, no shadda)
gates.then: emit decision=pending; prefer pending over the commoner reading.

gates.when: the initial letter is hamzat al-waṣl (ٱ) or a Form VII/VIII/X connecting alif
gates.then: strip it before root certification; never count it as a first radical; route the residual stem through ../rules/root-decision-rules.json.

gates.when: the only evidence is a norm() (lenient) match across a hamza collapse
gates.then: deny certification; this is exactly the case norm_never_certifies in ../rules/root-decision-rules.json was written for.
